channel.title: Projects on Dalhousie Ocean Dynamics Group (DODG)
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on Dalhousie Ocean Dynamics Group (DODG)

item.title: High latitude Ocean Mixing
item.description: We are interested in the physical processes that lead to turbulence and mixing at high latitudes, with a focus on the Arctic Ocean.

item.title: Internal waves
item.description: Internal waves are primarily generated by the winds and tides. Internal waves propagate and dissipate throughout the global ocean.

item.title: Marine Carbon Dioxide Removal
item.description: Marine Carbon Dioxide Removal aims to remove carbon dioxide from the atmosphere by storing it in the ocean. Techniques that we are interested include Ocean Alkalinity Enhancement and Direct Ocean Capture.

item.title: Near-surface turbulence and air-sea gas exchange
item.description:
Understanding uncertainties in estimates of air-sea gas exchange of CO$_2$

The rate of air-sea gas exchange for gasses such as CO$_2$ depend on both near-surface turbulence and the injection of bubbles into the surface layer of the ocean.

item.title: Wide Learning
item.description:
Et sensus uncos Troiaeque mori candidaque ostentis
